<!-- type: reference -->

# [Your Reference Title: "[Topic] Reference"]

> **When to use this template:** You're writing factual lookup material that someone refers to while working. References are terse, use consistent structure, and focus on facts and definitions. Good reference docs answer "What are the rules?" or "What are the available options?" They don't explain *why*.

## Overview

<!-- 
A one-paragraph definition or summary of what this reference covers.
Be precise and terse. No narrative.

Example:
"Naming conventions for JavaScript variables, functions, and files 
in production codebases. These rules apply to all repos in the 
javcb GitHub organizations."
-->

## [Category 1: Topic]

<!-- 
Use consistent structure for all categories.
Include:
- Clear heading
- Definition or rule
- Examples (in code block or list)
- When to use / when NOT to use
- Cross-references to related rules

Keep it factual. No explanation of why.
Example structure:

## Naming Conventions

**Rule:** Variable names use camelCase

**Format:**
const myVariableName = value;

**Examples:**
- ✅ userId
- ✅ isActive
- ❌ user_id (snake_case)
- ❌ UserID (PascalCase)

**When to use:** All JavaScript/TypeScript variable declarations

**Related:** See "Function Naming" below for function-specific rules
-->
